title: [Heliolisk] Battle Guide
summary: A sun-reliant special [sweeper] that turns 109 SpA and 109 Speed into a 160+ effective damage output through [solar-power].
strengths: [solar-power] raises effective SpA into the 160s under sun
Base 109 Speed beats nearly every unboosted [timid]-nature threat
[weather-ball] becomes a [Fire] attack in sun for [Grass] and [Steel] cove[rage]
weaknesses: [Fighting] and [Ground] both hit for 2x; [close-combat] or [earthquake] outright OHKOs

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Heliolisk] is the [Electric]/[Normal] frill-lizard built entirely a[round] hidden ability [solar-power]. Base 109 SpA and 109 Speed is a clean symmetric statline, and under sun the SpA modifier lifts damage output into the neighborhood of a genuine wallbreaker. The whole pitch of the card is that it trades bulk and longevity for one of the cleanest [Electric]/[Normal] offensive angles in the format.

## Strengths
Under sun, [thunderbolt] rolls through [Water] and [Flying] cores, and [hyper-voice] provides a second STAB that ignores both [substitute] and disguise. Base 109 Speed with a [timid] nature clears 357 actual Speed, outrunning every unboosted offensive threat that sits in the 100-108 bracket. [weather-ball] is the real [trick] — in sun it becomes a [Fire]-type move, meaning [Heliolisk] gets [Grass], [Steel], [Ice], and [Bug] cove[rage] off a [sing]le slot without a dedicated [Fire] move. [focus-blast] [round]s the set out with an answer to defensive [Rock] and [Steel] pivots.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Fighting] and [Ground] are both neutral-to-devastating matchups. 62 HP and 52 Def give [Heliolisk] no room to [absorb] anything, so any [close-combat] or [earthquake] that lands ends the sequence. [solar-power] ticks 1/8 HP every turn, which means the sun timer is effectively a death clock. Priority — [extreme-speed], [ice-shard], [aqua-jet] — is also structurally lethal once any chip damage accumulates. A mirror weather setter (oppo[sing] Rain, Sand, or Snow) erases the SpA modifier and leaves [Heliolisk] as a brittle mid-tier special attacker.

## Recommended Moveset
[thunderbolt] is the always-on STAB main. [hyper-voice] is picked as the second STAB specifically to break subs and disguise, because otherwise stall cores can cheese the [Electric]-only cove[rage]. [weather-ball] holds the cove[rage] slot because its sun-[Fire] [conversion] handles [Grass], [Steel], [Bug], and [Ice] off one button, letting the fourth slot stay flexible. [focus-blast] takes that fourth slot, accepting 70% accuracy because it's the only thing on the learnset that actually hurts physically defensive [Rock] or [Steel] switch-ins. If the team can't afford accuracy rolls, [dark-pulse] is the safer swap.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[focus-sash] is the default item. It guarantees one priority or quick-claw hit survived, which is typically the only window [Heliolisk] needs. [choice-scarf] is the alternative when sun uptime is unreliable and the team still needs a Speed-tier anchor; it also lets [Heliolisk] [revenge] scarfed threats. The ability must be the hidden [solar-power] — dry-skin is an entirely different defensive archetype. Tera [Electric] pushes [thunderbolt] into one-shot territory, and Tera [Ground] flips the [Ground] weakness while giving [Heliolisk] a mirror against oppo[sing] [Electric]s.

## Teammates & Synergy
A dedicated sun setter (Ninetales being the cleanest pick) is mandatory; [Heliolisk] has no ability-based weather of its own. The team also needs a [Ground] immunity pivot to cover [earthquake] switches, and a priority user or [Fighting]-resist pivot to cover the [Fighting] matchup. A [stealth-rock] lead multiplies [Heliolisk]'s effective damage by pushing 2HKOs into OHKOs and takes pressure off the [focus-blast] accuracy rolls.

## Play Patterns
Lead the sun setter on turn one, bring [Heliolisk] in on turn two behind a slow pivot or a free KO. Open with [thunderbolt] into anything neutral; save [weather-ball] for the obvious [Grass] or [Steel] switch-in; only c[lick] [focus-blast] when the accuracy roll won't lose the game. Tera early and aggressively — the moment a [close-combat] or [earthquake] is telegraphed, flipping the matchup is more valuable than holding the Tera for a mirror. [Heliolisk]'s sequence is short and sharp: two turns of damage, then either a KO or a swap.
